sections.about: This metric measures gross wages paid to female employees/workers as a percentage of total wages paid by the entity. It is disclosed under BRSR Principle 5 — Human Rights, Essential Indicators, remuneration/salary/wages. A higher value indicates better performance.
sections.performanceAndDrivers: FY2022-23 values are not available for any of the four companies. In FY2023-24, Tata leads at 7%, followed by SAIL at 6.37%, JSW at 5.22% and Jindal at 2.20%. In FY2024-25, SAIL leads at 6.19%, Tata is close at 6.00%, JSW is 5.15% and Jindal improves to 4.31%. Tata’s FY2024-25 BRSR notes an important methodology change: salaries to other-than-permanent employees/workers were included in the wage base, making FY2024-25 not fully comparable with the prior year.
sections.tataPositioning: In FY2024-25, Tata ranks 2nd at 6.00%, only 3.1% below SAIL’s 6.19%. Tata moved from rank 4 in FY2022-23 due to non-reporting, to rank 1 in FY2023-24, then to rank 2 in FY2024-25. The FY2024-25 fall from 7% to 6.00% should be read with caution because the wage base changed to include other-than-permanent employees/workers.
sections.comparabilityNotes: Tata’s FY2024-25 disclosure states that salaries to other-than-permanent employees/workers were included in the wage base, so FY2024-25 is not comparable to FY2023-24 to that extent.

flags.flagNote: Tata’s FY2024-25 trend is affected by a disclosed wage-scope methodology change.
